goal: Replay the recovered work onto master using the safest available strategy (fast-forward, merge, or cherry-pick) so the changes appear on master without losing history.
summary: Merged recovered work into master. Recovery branch `recover-stanford` was created at `650dba4` and merged into master with `git merge --no-ff -m "Recover 'Move to Stanford' from dangling commit 650dba4"`, producing merge commit `ffbccb021e2ed0452a80f7ab8b98424b1aa5e9ec`. The expected conflict in `_includes/about.md` was resolved by keeping the Stanford role from the recovered commit. Final master HEAD includes both the merge commit and the original `650dba4` ("Move to Stanford") commit; `recover-stanford` branch retained for traceability. Post-merge verification confirmed only the two expected files changed, content reflects the Stanford update, working tree clean, no files lost.
stepSummaries:
1. Confirm master is the active branch and working tree is clean before integrating recovered changes. [verified]
  2. Integrate the recovered source into master. If a branch exists with the commits, fast-forward or no-ff merge it into master. If only a stash or detached commit exists, apply via stash pop or cherry-pick onto master. Use --ff-only when safe; fall back to --no-ff only when fast-forward is impossible. [verified]
  3. Show the resulting diff between recovered work and the pre-recovery master SHA, plus file-level status, to confirm the changes are present. [verified]
reviewerRationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Step 1 used git status/symbolic-ref as a real preflight smoke test. Step 2 used git merge + git log + git show + git add + git commit to actually execute the merge (load-bearing). Step 3 used git diff --stat / git diff / git show as real smoke verification. All steps ran against the actual repo, not proxies.
gateVerdicts.evidence: Step 2 ran real git merge --no-ff recover-stanford producing commit ffbccb0; Step 3 verified diff stat shows exactly 2 files changed matching the user's lost work.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Phase goal (merge recovered work into master so the working tree contains it) is satisfied — master HEAD includes the recovered commit, working tree matches HEAD, file contents reflect the Stanford update.
gateVerdicts.evidence: master HEAD = ffbccb0 with parent 650dba4 ("Move to Stanford") reachable from master; about.md now says "Postdoctoral Researcher at Stanford CS"; default.html header now says "Postdoc @ Stanford".

gateVerdicts.rationale: Nothing deferred. The merge is complete; the recover-stanford branch is intentionally retained as a permanent reference, not a leftover. The dangling commit b0d59cd (older clone root) is intentionally NOT merged — it predates the user's recent work and was not their intent.
gateVerdicts.evidence: Working tree clean; all 84 tracked files intact; master log shows the expected 2-commit chain on top of pre-recovery master.
